propositions: A single-instance Python project service automates rebase-to-tip then ancestor-atomic submit for the front submittable Autosubmit change/chain, serializing to prevent parallel agent sessions racing.
The loop rebases the front change to the current tip, awaits a fresh Verified +1, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA, holding a single in-flight wipChain.
A fixed 15s poll interval picks up new Autosubmit labels while staying gentle on the Gerrit REST API, distinct from land's 30s client poll, and writes a liveness heartbeat once per poll.

Under FFO/ADR-0040, Gerrit submittable already requires descendant-of-tip, so a behind-tip change is non-submittable, but a native submittable=false does not distinguish behind-tip from a failing vote.
The bot reads existing Verified votes only and casts no votes, preserving the invariant that the tested tree equals the merged tree across all children.
Runtime credentials and Gerrit capabilities (Submit ACL, rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader, SSM token) are provisioned by S5 at deploy time as runtime preconditions, not a build-order dependency.
